text.title: decision-tree-cloud-provider Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for decision-tree-cloud-provider as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommends multi-cloud by default as "best practice" without assessing the operational overhead.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-cloud doubles identity, networking, and cost-management complexity. It is rarely justified for teams under 50 engineers. The tree defaults to single-provider unless explicit goals (DR, best-of-breed) justify it.

failure-mode.description: Agent ignores existing technology investments and jumps straight to workload type.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: BYOL savings (Azure for Microsoft shops) and integration costs (switching identity providers) are often larger than the marginal service cost difference. Existing investments must be the first gate.
